title: Calonema plicatum
taxonomy: Calonema plicatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Calonema, which is a group of orchids found in Southeast Asia. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that consists of over 30,000 species. These plants are known for their beautiful and intricate flowers, which often have unique shapes and colors. Calonema plicatum, in particular, is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, where it grows in the wild.

intro: Calonema plicatum, also known as Indian gentian or Gentiana plicata, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that can aid in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Calonema plicatum include gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and amarogentin, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Calonema plicatum is used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and gastrointestinal disorders.
